examples.en: Sales doubled this quarter. Moreover, customer retention reached an all-time high.
examples.zh: 本季銷售翻倍。此外客戶留存率達到歷史新高。

toeic.pattern: Emphatic negation with by no means (強調否定：絕不)
toeic.meaning_zh: by no means 表示「絕不」，加強否定語氣。
toeic.structure: S + be / V + by no means + ... (或置句首倒裝)

examples.en: The decision was by no means easy for the executive team.
examples.zh: 這個決定對主管團隊來說絕非容易。

examples.en: By no means should confidential data be shared with third parties.
examples.zh: 機密資料絕不應與第三方分享。

toeic.pattern: Emphatic negation with in no way (強調否定：毫不)
toeic.meaning_zh: in no way 表示「絕不、毫不」，置句首時主句倒裝。
toeic.structure: In no way + 助動詞 + S + V

examples.en: In no way does this delay affect our long-term commitment to clients.
examples.zh: 此延誤絕不影響我們對客戶的長期承諾。

examples.en: In no way will the company tolerate violations of compliance rules.
examples.zh: 公司絕不容忍違反合規規範的行為。

toeic.pattern: Double negative: not...without (雙重否定)
toeic.meaning_zh: 雙重否定表達肯定語氣，常用於正式商業書寫。
toeic.structure: S + not + V + without + V-ing

examples.en: We cannot approve the budget without consulting the finance committee.
examples.zh: 未諮詢財務委員會我們無法批准預算。

examples.en: The CEO never signs a contract without thoroughly reviewing all clauses.
examples.zh: 執行長從不在未徹底審閱條款前簽署合約。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: no less than (多達)
toeic.meaning_zh: no less than 表示「不少於、多達」，強調數量多。
toeic.structure: no less than + 數量

examples.en: No less than fifty representatives attended the international supplier summit.
examples.zh: 多達五十位代表出席了國際供應商峰會。

examples.en: The company invested no less than ten million dollars in research.
examples.zh: 公司在研發上投入多達一千萬美元。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: not so much A as B (與其說是 A 不如說是 B)
toeic.meaning_zh: 與其說是 A，不如說是 B，用於修正或強調。
toeic.structure: not so much + A + as + B

examples.en: Success comes not so much from talent as from consistent effort.
examples.zh: 成功與其說來自才能，不如說來自持續努力。

examples.en: Their failure was not so much a strategy issue as an execution problem.
examples.zh: 他們的失敗與其說是策略問題，不如說是執行問題。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: as many...as (與一樣多)
toeic.meaning_zh: as many + 可數名詞 + as，比較數量相等。
toeic.structure: as many + N + as + ...

examples.en: Our branch processes as many orders as the headquarters does daily.
examples.zh: 我們分公司每天處理的訂單量與總部一樣多。

examples.en: We received as many applications as we did last hiring season.
examples.zh: 我們收到的申請數量與上一招募季一樣多。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: the + comparative + of the two (兩者中較的那一個)
toeic.meaning_zh: 兩者之中較…的那一個，要用「the + 比較級」。
toeic.structure: the + 比較級 + of the two + N

examples.en: The taller of the two candidates also has stronger leadership skills.
examples.zh: 兩位候選人中較高的那位也具備更強的領導力。

examples.en: We selected the cheaper of the two suppliers for the trial order.
examples.zh: 我們選了兩家供應商中較便宜的進行試訂單。

toeic.pattern: Each of + plural N + singular V (each of 後接複數名詞動詞用單數)
toeic.meaning_zh: each of 後接複數名詞，動詞用單數。
toeic.structure: Each of + the + 複數 N + 單數 V

examples.en: Each of our regional offices reports directly to the global headquarters.
examples.zh: 我們每個區域辦公室都直接向全球總部回報。

examples.en: Each of the proposals deserves careful review by senior management.
examples.zh: 每份提案都值得高階管理層仔細審閱。

toeic.pattern: Neither of + plural N + singular V (neither of 後接複數名詞動詞用單數)
toeic.meaning_zh: neither of 後接複數名詞，動詞通常用單數。
toeic.structure: Neither of + the + 複數 N + 單數 V

examples.en: Neither of the proposals meets our minimum profitability requirements.
examples.zh: 兩份提案都未達我們的最低獲利要求。

examples.en: Neither of the candidates has prior experience in financial auditing.
examples.zh: 兩位候選人都沒有先前的財務稽核經驗。

toeic.pattern: The number of vs a number of (the number of 與 a number of)
toeic.meaning_zh: the number of 動詞用單數；a number of 動詞用複數。
toeic.structure: The number of + N (單數 V) / A number of + N (複數 V)

examples.en: The number of subscribers has tripled since the platform was relaunched.
examples.zh: 自平台重新上線以來，訂閱人數已增為三倍。

examples.en: A number of employees are participating in the leadership workshop.
examples.zh: 有許多員工正參與領導力工作坊。

toeic.pattern: Look forward to + V-ing (期待做某事)
toeic.meaning_zh: look forward to 後接動名詞，表示期待。
toeic.structure: S + look forward to + V-ing

examples.en: We look forward to receiving your detailed proposal by next Friday.
examples.zh: 我們期待在下週五前收到您的詳細提案。

examples.en: The team looks forward to collaborating with the new business partners.
examples.zh: 團隊期待與新事業夥伴合作。

toeic.pattern: Be used to + V-ing (習慣於)
toeic.meaning_zh: be used to + V-ing 表示「習慣於做某事」。
toeic.structure: S + be used to + V-ing / N

examples.en: Our sales team is used to handling international clients across time zones.
examples.zh: 我們業務團隊習慣處理跨時區的國際客戶。

examples.en: She is used to negotiating contracts under tight deadlines.
examples.zh: 她習慣在緊迫期限下談判合約。

toeic.pattern: Would rather...than (寧可也不)
toeic.meaning_zh: would rather + 原形動詞 + than + 原形動詞，表示偏好。
toeic.structure: S + would rather + V (原形) + than + V (原形)

examples.en: The CEO would rather delay launch than release an unfinished product.
examples.zh: 執行長寧可延後上市也不願推出未完成的產品。

examples.en: Most clients would rather pay a premium than risk poor service.
examples.zh: 多數客戶寧願多付一些也不願冒險使用差勁的服務。

toeic.pattern: Be dedicated to + V-ing (致力於)
toeic.meaning_zh: be dedicated/committed to 後接動名詞，表示致力。
toeic.structure: S + be dedicated / committed to + V-ing

examples.en: Our company is dedicated to providing exceptional customer service worldwide.
examples.zh: 本公司致力於提供全球卓越的顧客服務。

examples.en: The team is committed to meeting every project milestone on schedule.
examples.zh: 團隊致力於按時達成每個專案里程碑。

toeic.pattern: Object to + V-ing (反對)
toeic.meaning_zh: object to 後接動名詞，表示反對做某事。
toeic.structure: S + object to + V-ing

examples.en: Several board members objected to outsourcing the entire IT department.
examples.zh: 數位董事反對將整個 IT 部門外包。

examples.en: Employees objected to working overtime without additional compensation.
examples.zh: 員工反對在無額外補償下加班。

toeic.pattern: Provided / providing that (假如)
toeic.meaning_zh: provided/providing that 引導條件子句，表示「假如、只要」。
toeic.structure: Provided that + S + V, S + V

examples.en: Provided that all conditions are met, the merger will proceed in June.
examples.zh: 假如所有條件皆滿足，併購案將於六月進行。

examples.en: Providing that the budget is approved, we can hire two new analysts.
examples.zh: 只要預算獲批，我們就能聘請兩位新分析師。

toeic.pattern: Unless (除非)
toeic.meaning_zh: unless 等於 if not，引導否定條件子句。
toeic.structure: Unless + S + V, S + V

examples.en: Unless the supplier confirms delivery, we will cancel the entire order.
examples.zh: 除非供應商確認交貨，否則我們將取消整筆訂單。

examples.en: Unless margins improve, the board may reconsider the expansion plan.
examples.zh: 除非利潤率改善，董事會可能重新考慮擴張計畫。

toeic.pattern: In case + S + V (以防萬一)
toeic.meaning_zh: in case 引導預防性條件子句。
toeic.structure: S + V, in case + S + V

examples.en: Please back up all files in case the server fails again.
examples.zh: 請備份所有檔案以防伺服器再次故障。

examples.en: Bring extra copies of the contract in case the client needs them.
examples.zh: 請多帶幾份合約以防客戶需要。

toeic.pattern: As long as (只要)
toeic.meaning_zh: as long as 表示條件「只要…就…」。
toeic.structure: As long as + S + V, S + V

examples.en: As long as the deadline is met, the team can choose its hours.
examples.zh: 只要能準時達成期限，團隊可以自選工時。

examples.en: Customers will stay loyal as long as quality remains consistently high.
examples.zh: 只要品質持續優異，顧客就會保持忠誠。

toeic.pattern: It takes + sb + time + to + V (某人做某事所需的時間)
toeic.meaning_zh: 表示某人完成某事所需的時間。
toeic.structure: It takes + sb + 時間 + to + V

examples.en: It takes the team about three weeks to prepare the annual report.
examples.zh: 團隊大約需要三週時間準備年度報告。

examples.en: It took us six months to negotiate the new distribution agreement.
examples.zh: 我們花了六個月談判新的經銷協議。

toeic.pattern: Spend + time / money + in V-ing (花費時間或金錢做某事，in 可省略)
toeic.meaning_zh: spend + 時間/金錢 + (in) + V-ing，表示花費。
toeic.structure: S + spend + 時間 / 金錢 + (in) + V-ing

examples.en: The company spends millions of dollars improving cybersecurity each year.
examples.zh: 公司每年花費數百萬美元提升網路安全。

examples.en: Managers spend hours reviewing applications during the hiring season.
examples.zh: 經理在招募季要花數小時審閱應徵資料。

toeic.pattern: There is no point in V-ing (做某事沒有意義)
toeic.meaning_zh: 做某事沒有意義或必要。
toeic.structure: There is no point in + V-ing

examples.en: There is no point in delaying the announcement any further.
examples.zh: 繼續延後公告毫無意義。

examples.en: There is no point in arguing once the contract has been signed.
examples.zh: 合約一旦簽署，再爭論也沒有意義。

toeic.pattern: Be on the verge of + V-ing (即將)
toeic.meaning_zh: be on the verge of 表示「瀕臨、即將」。
toeic.structure: S + be on the verge of + V-ing / N

examples.en: The company is on the verge of finalizing a strategic partnership.
examples.zh: 公司即將敲定一項策略合作關係。

examples.en: Our division is on the verge of launching a revolutionary product.
examples.zh: 我們部門即將推出革命性的產品。

toeic.pattern: Be likely to + V (可能)
toeic.meaning_zh: be likely to 表示「有可能做某事」。
toeic.structure: S + be likely to + V

examples.en: Interest rates are likely to rise again in the coming quarter.
examples.zh: 利率在下一季可能再次調升。

examples.en: Consumers are likely to spend more during the upcoming holiday season.
examples.zh: 消費者在即將到來的假期季可能會花更多錢。

toeic.pattern: Be supposed to + V (應該)
toeic.meaning_zh: be supposed to 表示根據規定或預期應該做某事。
toeic.structure: S + be supposed to + V

examples.en: All visitors are supposed to register at the front desk first.
examples.zh: 所有訪客都應先在前台登記。

examples.en: Managers are supposed to submit quarterly reviews by the end of June.
examples.zh: 經理應在六月底前提交季度檢討報告。

toeic.pattern: Cannot help + V-ing (忍不住)
toeic.meaning_zh: cannot help + V-ing 表示「忍不住做某事」。
toeic.structure: S + cannot help + V-ing

examples.en: Clients cannot help admiring our team's professionalism and attention to detail.
examples.zh: 客戶不禁讚賞我們團隊的專業與細節重視。

examples.en: I cannot help feeling optimistic about the upcoming product launch.
examples.zh: 我對即將到來的產品發表會不禁感到樂觀。

toeic.pattern: Had better + V (最好做某事)
toeic.meaning_zh: had better + 原形動詞，建議或勸告做某事。
toeic.structure: S + had better + (not) + V (原形)

examples.en: We had better confirm the booking before the trade show begins.
examples.zh: 我們最好在貿易展開始前確認訂位。

examples.en: The CFO had better review the figures before tomorrow's board meeting.
examples.zh: 財務長最好在明天董事會議前再次審視這些數字。

toeic.pattern: May / might as well + V (不妨做某事)
toeic.meaning_zh: may/might as well 表示「不妨、倒不如」。
toeic.structure: S + may / might as well + V (原形)

examples.en: We might as well postpone the launch until quality issues are resolved.
examples.zh: 我們不妨延後發表，等品質問題解決再說。

examples.en: Since the office is closed, we may as well work from home.
examples.zh: 既然辦公室關閉，我們不妨在家工作。

toeic.pattern: It is high time + S + past V (早該做某事了)
toeic.meaning_zh: It is high time 後接過去式，表示「早該做某事」。
toeic.structure: It is high time + (that) + S + V (過去式)

examples.en: It is high time the company updated its outdated marketing strategy.
examples.zh: 公司早該更新過時的行銷策略了。

examples.en: It is high time we invested in renewable energy for our facilities.
examples.zh: 我們早該為廠房投資再生能源了。

toeic.pattern: Inversion with little (否定副詞 little 句首倒裝)
toeic.meaning_zh: 否定副詞 little 置句首時主句倒裝，表示「絲毫不、幾乎不」。
toeic.structure: Little + 助動詞 + S + V

examples.en: Little did the investors know that the startup would dominate the market.
examples.zh: 投資人絲毫不知這家新創公司將主宰市場。

examples.en: Little did we expect that the merger would face regulatory opposition.
examples.zh: 我們完全沒料到併購案會遭遇監管機構反對。

toeic.pattern: Future continuous for polite inquiry (未來進行式委婉詢問)
toeic.meaning_zh: 用未來進行式 (will be + V-ing) 委婉詢問對方計畫，語氣比 will 更客氣、不帶催促，常見於商務溝通探詢對方安排。
toeic.structure: S + will be + V-ing (進行式)+ ?

examples.en: Will you be attending the quarterly review next Friday?
examples.zh: 您下週五會參加季度檢討會嗎？

examples.en: Will the finance team be submitting the figures before noon?
examples.zh: 財務團隊會在中午前提交數據嗎？

toeic.pattern: Present perfect with stative verbs: duration (狀態動詞現在完成式表持續)
toeic.meaning_zh: 狀態動詞 (know/own/belong/have) 用現在完成式而非完成進行式來表示從過去持續至今的狀態長度，常搭配 for/since。
toeic.structure: S + have / has + p.p. (狀態動詞)+ for / since + 時間

examples.en: We have known the supplier since the company was founded.
examples.zh: 自從公司創立以來，我們就認識這家供應商。

examples.en: She has owned the franchise for over a decade.
examples.zh: 她擁有這家加盟店已超過十年。

toeic.pattern: Past perfect in reported backshift (間接引述往前推之過去完成式)
toeic.meaning_zh: 間接引述中，原本的現在完成式或過去式往前推 (backshift) 成過去完成式，表示在報導時間點之前已完成的動作。
toeic.structure: S + said (that) + S + had + p.p. (過去完成式)

examples.en: The manager said the shipment had already left the warehouse.
examples.zh: 經理說貨物已經離開倉庫了。

examples.en: They reported that sales had dropped before the merger.
examples.zh: 他們報告說銷售在合併前就已下滑。

toeic.pattern: Future perfect for deadline completion (未來完成式表截止前完成)
toeic.meaning_zh: 未來完成式 (will have + p.p.) 強調在未來某個截止時間點之前某事將已完成，商務常搭配 by + 時間。
toeic.structure: S + will have + p.p. (完成式)+ by + 未來時間

examples.en: By the end of this quarter, we will have onboarded fifty new clients.
examples.zh: 到本季結束時，我們將已招攬五十位新客戶。

examples.en: The contractor will have finished the renovation by Monday.
examples.zh: 承包商到週一前就會完成翻修。

toeic.pattern: Historic / narrative present (歷史現在式 / 敘事現在式)
toeic.meaning_zh: 用現在簡單式敘述過去已發生的事件以增添臨場感，常見於簡報、案例分享與新聞標題式陳述。
toeic.structure: S + V (現在式)+ ... (描述過去事件)

examples.en: In 2010, the founder launches the firm with just three employees.
examples.zh: 二〇一〇年，創辦人僅以三名員工起家創立公司。

examples.en: The negotiator walks in, presents the figures, and the deal closes within an hour.
examples.zh: 談判代表走進來、提出數字，交易在一小時內就成交。

toeic.pattern: Modal shall for obligation in contracts (合約中 shall 表約束性義務)
toeic.meaning_zh: 在合約與正式文件中，shall 用來表示具約束力的義務或規定 (= must)，主詞常為第三人稱。
toeic.structure: S + shall + V (原形) (契約義務)

examples.en: The tenant shall pay the rent on the first day of each month.
examples.zh: 承租人應於每月一日繳納租金。

examples.en: Either party shall provide thirty days' written notice before termination.
examples.zh: 任一方應於終止前提供三十天書面通知。

toeic.pattern: Modal will for habitual / characteristic behavior (will 表習慣性或特性行為)
toeic.meaning_zh: will 不僅指未來，也可表示主詞典型、可預期的習慣性行為或特性 (= 通常會)。
toeic.structure: S + will + V (原形) (慣性行為)

examples.en: Our most loyal customers will often recommend us to colleagues.
examples.zh: 我們最忠實的客戶通常會把我們推薦給同事。

examples.en: A good account manager will always follow up within a day.
examples.zh: 優秀的客戶經理總是會在一天內跟進。

toeic.pattern: Modal would for past repeated actions (would 表過去反覆動作)
toeic.meaning_zh: would + 原形可描述過去反覆發生的習慣動作 (與 used to 近義，但僅限動作非狀態)，常用於回憶敘述。
toeic.structure: S + would + V (原形動作) (過去習慣)

examples.en: During the start-up days, the team would meet every morning at seven.
examples.zh: 創業初期，團隊每天早上七點都會開會。

examples.en: Before the system upgrade, staff would file every report by hand.
examples.zh: 系統升級前，員工都會手寫每一份報告。

toeic.pattern: Modal need not vs don't have to (need not 與 don't have to 表無必要)
toeic.meaning_zh: need not / don't have to 皆表示沒有必要 (無義務)，與 must not (禁止) 意義截然不同；need 作助動詞時後接原形且無 to。
toeic.structure: S + need not + V (原形) / S + don't have to + V (原形) (無必要)

examples.en: You need not submit the form again if you applied last week.
examples.zh: 如果您上週已申請，就不必再次提交表格。

examples.en: Employees don't have to attend the optional webinar.
examples.zh: 員工不必參加這場非強制的網路研討會。

toeic.pattern: Modal should for logical expectation (should 表合理預期)
toeic.meaning_zh: should 除表建議外，也可表示根據常理推斷的合理預期 (應該會)，不含義務含義。
toeic.structure: S + should + V (原形) (合理預期)

examples.en: The package should arrive by Thursday if it shipped today.
examples.zh: 如果今天出貨，包裹應該會在週四前送達。

examples.en: With these numbers, the proposal should pass the board easily.
examples.zh: 以這些數字來看，提案應該能輕鬆通過董事會。

toeic.pattern: Modal can / could for theoretical possibility (can / could 表理論上可能性)
toeic.meaning_zh: can 表示一般理論上可能發生的情況 (普遍性)，could 則指特定情境下的可能性，二者語氣不同。
toeic.structure: S + can / could + V (原形) (可能性)

examples.en: Delays in customs can affect the entire delivery schedule.
examples.zh: 海關延誤可能影響整個交貨時程。

examples.en: A sudden rate change could disrupt our cash flow this month.
examples.zh: 突如其來的利率變動可能在本月打亂我們的現金流。

toeic.pattern: Modal might for tentative suggestion (might 表委婉建議)
toeic.meaning_zh: might (you might want to / it might be) 用來提出非常委婉、保留的建議，降低強制感，常見於職場給回饋。
toeic.structure: S + might want to + V (原形) / It might be + adj + to + V (委婉建議)

examples.en: You might want to double-check the figures before the client call.
examples.zh: 在與客戶通話前，您或許可以再核對一下數字。

examples.en: It might be wise to delay the launch until testing is complete.
examples.zh: 在測試完成前延後上市或許比較明智。

toeic.pattern: Modal must for logical deduction: present (must 表現在的肯定推斷)
toeic.meaning_zh: must 表示根據現有證據推斷出的高度肯定結論 (一定是)，用於現在情境，與表義務的 must 區分。
toeic.structure: S + must + be / V (原形) (肯定推論)

examples.en: The lights are still on, so someone must be working late.
examples.zh: 燈還亮著，所以一定有人在加班。

examples.en: Sales have tripled; the new strategy must be working.
examples.zh: 銷售翻了三倍，新策略一定奏效了。

toeic.pattern: Modal can't for negative deduction (can't 表否定推斷不可能)
toeic.meaning_zh: can't / cannot 表示根據邏輯推斷不可能的否定結論 (不可能)，是 must (肯定推論) 的否定對應。
toeic.structure: S + can't / cannot + be / V (原形) (否定推論)

examples.en: They can't be serious about cutting the entire budget.
examples.zh: 他們不可能認真要砍掉整筆預算。

examples.en: This invoice cannot be correct; the totals don't match.
examples.zh: 這張發票不可能正確，總額對不上。

toeic.pattern: Passive with get: get + p.p. (get 被動語態)
toeic.meaning_zh: 口語商務中以 get + p.p. 代替 be + p.p.，常帶有事件突發、出乎意料或對主詞造成影響的含義。
toeic.structure: S + get / got + p.p. (get 被動)

examples.en: Two of our top engineers got promoted last month.
examples.zh: 我們兩位頂尖工程師上個月獲得晉升。

examples.en: The shipment got delayed because of the port strike.
examples.zh: 由於港口罷工，這批貨被延誤了。

toeic.pattern: Passive reporting structure: it is said that / be said to (被動轉述句型)
toeic.meaning_zh: 用 It is said/believed/reported that 或 S + be said/believed to + V 來客觀轉述普遍說法，避免指明消息來源，常見於報導。
toeic.structure: It is + said / believed / reported + that + S + V / S + be + said + to + V (原形)

examples.en: It is believed that the merger will create two thousand jobs.
examples.zh: 據信這樁合併案將創造兩千個工作機會。

examples.en: The CEO is said to be considering an early retirement.
examples.zh: 據說執行長正在考慮提早退休。

toeic.pattern: Passive with double object verbs (雙受詞動詞被動)
toeic.meaning_zh: give/offer/send/award 等雙受詞動詞變被動時，間接受詞 (人) 通常作被動主詞，直接受詞 (物) 留在後面。
toeic.structure: S (人)+ be + p.p. + 直接受詞 (物) (雙受詞被動)

examples.en: Each participant was given a detailed feedback report.
examples.zh: 每位與會者都拿到一份詳細的回饋報告。

examples.en: The top performer was offered a relocation package.
examples.zh: 表現最佳者獲得一份外派搬遷方案。

toeic.pattern: Passive with prepositional verbs (片語動詞被動)
toeic.meaning_zh: deal with / look into / refer to 等片語動詞變被動時，介系詞必須保留在動詞後面不可省略。
toeic.structure: S + be + p.p. + prep (介系詞保留) (片語動詞被動)

examples.en: The complaint will be dealt with by customer service today.
examples.zh: 這項客訴今天會由客服處理。

examples.en: The discrepancy is being looked into by the audit team.
examples.zh: 這項差異正由稽核團隊調查中。

toeic.pattern: Causative passive: have / get something done for services (使役被動表安排服務)
toeic.meaning_zh: have/get + 受詞 + p.p. 表示安排他人完成某事 (找人做)，強調服務由第三方執行而非主詞親為。
toeic.structure: S + have / get + 受詞 (物)+ p.p. (使役被動)

examples.en: We had the conference room repainted before the client visit.
examples.zh: 客戶來訪前，我們找人把會議室重新粉刷了。

examples.en: You should get the contract reviewed by a lawyer first.
examples.zh: 你應該先請律師審閱合約。

toeic.pattern: Conditional with were to: hypothetical future (were to 假設未來條件句)
toeic.meaning_zh: If S + were to + V 用來假設未來不太可能或純屬假想的情況，語氣比第二類條件句更正式、更具假設性。
toeic.structure: If + S + were to + V (原形), S + would + V (原形)

examples.en: If the deal were to fall through, we would still have a backup supplier.
examples.zh: 萬一這筆交易告吹，我們仍有備用供應商。

examples.en: If the company were to expand overseas, fluency in Mandarin would be essential.
examples.zh: 假使公司要拓展海外，中文流利將是必要條件。

toeic.pattern: Conditional with happen to: chance condition (happen to 偶然條件句)
toeic.meaning_zh: If S + happen to + V 表示假如碰巧發生某事，強調偶然性，常用於委婉提出可能情況。
toeic.structure: If + S + happen to + V (原形), ...

examples.en: If you happen to see the regional director, please pass on the message.
examples.zh: 如果你碰巧見到區域總監，請幫忙轉達訊息。

examples.en: Should you happen to need assistance, our team is on standby.
examples.zh: 萬一您碰巧需要協助，我們團隊隨時待命。

toeic.pattern: Implied / reduced conditional without if (隱含或省略 if 的條件句)
toeic.meaning_zh: 用 otherwise / but for / with / without 等詞隱含條件，省略 if 子句，語意上仍構成條件句。
toeic.structure: With / Without / But for + N, S + would (have) + V (隱含條件)

examples.en: But for the board's support, the project would have been cancelled.
examples.zh: 要不是董事會的支持，這項專案早就被取消了。

examples.en: Without your timely intervention, we would have lost the account.
examples.zh: 若沒有你及時介入，我們早就失去這個客戶了。

toeic.pattern: Conditional with in the event that (in the event that 條件句)
toeic.meaning_zh: in the event that (= if) 是正式書面用語，用於合約或公告中陳述某情況一旦發生時的應對措施。
toeic.structure: In the event that + S + V, S + will + V (原形)

examples.en: In the event that the flight is cancelled, the company will cover rebooking costs.
examples.zh: 萬一航班取消，公司將承擔改訂機票的費用。

examples.en: In the event that payment is late, a surcharge will apply.
examples.zh: 如有逾期付款情形，將收取附加費。

toeic.pattern: Reported speech with modal backshift (間接引述情態助動詞往後推)
toeic.meaning_zh: 間接引述中情態助動詞會往後推:will→would、can→could、may→might；而 should/would/could/might 通常維持不變。
toeic.structure: S + said (that) + S + would / could / might + V (原形)

examples.en: She said she would forward the revised quotation by noon.
examples.zh: 她說她中午前會轉寄修改後的報價單。

examples.en: He told us he might join the call from the airport.
examples.zh: 他告訴我們他可能會從機場加入通話。

toeic.pattern: Reported speech: time and place shifts (間接引述時間地點指示語轉換)
toeic.meaning_zh: 間接引述時，時間地點指示語須轉換:now→then、today→that day、tomorrow→the next day、here→there、this→that。
toeic.structure: S + said (that) + ... + then / that day / the next day / there (指示語轉換)

examples.en: The supplier said the goods would be ready the following day.
examples.zh: 供應商說貨品隔天就會備妥。

examples.en: She mentioned that the meeting had been moved to that afternoon.
examples.zh: 她提到會議已改到那天下午。

toeic.pattern: Reported speech: yes / no questions with if / whether (轉述是非問句用 if / whether)
toeic.meaning_zh: 轉述是非問句時用 ask + if/whether 引導，並還原成肯定句語序 (主詞在前、動詞在後)，不用問號。
toeic.structure: S + asked + if / whether + S + V (陳述語序)

examples.en: The interviewer asked whether I had managed a remote team before.
examples.zh: 面試官問我之前是否管理過遠端團隊。

examples.en: They asked if the prototype would be ready for the trade show.
examples.zh: 他們問原型是否會在展會前準備好。

toeic.pattern: Reported speech: wh-questions (轉述 wh-問句)
toeic.meaning_zh: 轉述 wh-問句時保留疑問詞，但改成陳述句語序、去掉助動詞 do/does/did，並隨主句時態做時態後推。
toeic.structure: S + asked + 疑問詞 + S + V (陳述語序,無 do / does / did)

examples.en: The client asked when the final invoice would be issued.
examples.zh: 客戶問最終發票何時會開立。

examples.en: My manager asked why the figures differed from last quarter's.
examples.zh: 我的主管問為何數字與上季不同。

toeic.pattern: Reporting verbs with gerund / infinitive patterns (轉述動詞接動名詞或不定詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 不同轉述動詞接不同結構:suggest/admit + V-ing、offer/promise/refuse + to V、advise/remind/warn + sb + to V，選錯結構即錯。
toeic.structure: S + suggested + V-ing / promised + to + V / advised + sb + to + V

examples.en: The consultant suggested outsourcing the payroll function.
examples.zh: 顧問建議將薪資作業外包。

examples.en: They promised to deliver the components ahead of schedule.
examples.zh: 他們承諾提前交付零組件。

toeic.pattern: Subjunctive were after as if / as though (as if / as though 後接 were 假設語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: as if/as though 後接 were (含 I/he/she/it) 表示與現實不符的比擬，屬假設語氣，而非陳述真實情況。
toeic.structure: S + V + as if / as though + S + were (假設)

examples.en: He talks as if he were already the department head.
examples.zh: 他講話彷彿自己已經是部門主管似的。

examples.en: The intern handled the crisis as though she were a veteran manager.
examples.zh: 那位實習生處理危機的樣子彷彿她是資深經理一般。

toeic.pattern: Subjunctive after it is high time (it is high time 後接假設語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: It is time / It is high time + S + 過去式動詞，表示某事早該發生卻還沒做，過去式表假設語氣而非真正過去。
toeic.structure: It is (high) time + S + 過去式 V (假設語氣)

examples.en: It is high time the company upgraded its outdated software.
examples.zh: 公司早該升級這套過時的軟體了。

examples.en: It is time we reconsidered our pricing strategy.
examples.zh: 我們該重新考慮定價策略了。

toeic.pattern: Subjunctive after would rather: someone else (would rather 接他人之假設語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: would rather + 主詞 + 過去式動詞，表示寧願別人做某事 (與現在或未來相反的願望)，屬假設語氣。
toeic.structure: S + would rather (that) + S + 過去式 V (假設願望)

examples.en: I would rather you submitted the report by email instead.
examples.zh: 我寧願你改用電子郵件提交報告。

examples.en: The board would rather the launch took place next quarter.
examples.zh: 董事會寧願上市延到下一季進行。

toeic.pattern: Subjunctive in formal set phrases (正式固定片語中的假設語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: 少數固定片語保留古體假設語氣動詞原形，如 Be that as it may、Come what may、So be it、Suffice it to say，語氣正式。
toeic.structure: 固定片語 + V (原形) (假設語氣固定用法)

examples.en: Be that as it may, we must still meet the regulatory deadline.
examples.zh: 話雖如此，我們仍必須趕上法規期限。

examples.en: Suffice it to say the negotiations were far from smooth.
examples.zh: 總之這場談判遠不順利。

toeic.pattern: Subjunctive after verbs of ordering: order / command / direct / require (命令類動詞後的假設語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: order/command/direct/require 等命令類動詞後的 that 子句用原形動詞 (虛擬語氣)，與 demand/insist 同屬此類。
toeic.structure: S + order / require / direct + that + S + V (原形)

examples.en: The auditor required that all receipts be retained for seven years.
examples.zh: 稽核員要求所有收據須保存七年。

examples.en: Head office directed that every branch submit weekly figures.
examples.zh: 總部指示每家分行須提交每週數據。

toeic.pattern: Present perfect vs simple past: time reference (現在完成式與過去簡單式之時間參照)
toeic.meaning_zh: 未指明明確過去時間或與現在有關連用現在完成式；附有 yesterday/last week/ago 等明確過去時間則用簡單過去式，二者不可混用。
toeic.structure: S + have / has + p.p. (無明確時間) vs S + 過去式 V + 過去時間副詞

examples.en: We have finalized the budget, so we can proceed.
examples.zh: 我們已經敲定預算，所以可以著手進行。

examples.en: We finalized the budget last Friday after a long meeting.
examples.zh: 我們上週五在一場冗長會議後敲定了預算。

toeic.pattern: Modal perfect needn't have + p.p.: unnecessary past action (needn't have 表過去白做)
toeic.meaning_zh: needn't have + p.p. 表示過去做了某件其實沒必要做的事 (已做但白做)，與 didn't need to (沒做也沒必要) 含義不同。
toeic.structure: S + needn't have + p.p. (過去多此一舉)

examples.en: You needn't have rushed the report; the deadline was extended.
examples.zh: 你不必趕著做報告的，期限已經延長了。

examples.en: We needn't have booked the larger venue after all.
examples.zh: 結果我們根本不必訂那個比較大的場地。

toeic.pattern: Modal perfect would have + p.p.: unrealized past (would have 表過去未實現)
toeic.meaning_zh: would have + p.p. 表示過去原本會發生但實際沒發生的動作，常隱含一個未成立的條件 (第三類條件主句)。
toeic.structure: S + would have + p.p. (過去未實現)

examples.en: I would have joined the call, but my flight was delayed.
examples.zh: 我本來會加入通話的，但我的班機誤點了。

examples.en: They would have accepted the offer with slightly better terms.
examples.zh: 條件再好一點的話，他們本來會接受這項提議。

toeic.pattern: Modal may / might for concession (may / might 表讓步)
toeic.meaning_zh: may/might 可表示讓步 (= 雖然)，常與 but 連用構成 may...but... 句型，先承認一點再轉折。
toeic.structure: S + may / might + V (原形)..., but + S + V (讓步)

examples.en: The plan may sound ambitious, but it is entirely achievable.
examples.zh: 這計畫聽起來或許野心勃勃，但完全可以達成。

examples.en: The software might be expensive, but it saves hours of work.
examples.zh: 這軟體或許昂貴，但能節省大量工時。

toeic.pattern: Conditional as long as / so long as: sole condition (as long as 表唯一條件)
toeic.meaning_zh: so long as / as long as 強調唯一條件 (只要…就…)，語氣比 if 更強調該條件為必要前提，屬真實條件句。
toeic.structure: As long as + S + V (現在式), S + will + V (原形)

examples.en: As long as we hit the targets, the bonus will be paid in full.
examples.zh: 只要我們達標，獎金就會全額發放。

examples.en: So long as the funds clear today, shipping starts tomorrow.
examples.zh: 只要款項今天入帳，明天就開始出貨。

toeic.pattern: Subject-verb agreement with intervening phrases (插入片語時的主謂一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: 主詞與動詞之間插入修飾片語時，動詞仍須與真正的主詞一致，不受插入名詞影響。
toeic.structure: S (真主詞) + (修飾片語: of / along with / together with + N) + V (與真主詞一致) (動詞依真主詞單複數)

examples.en: The list of approved vendors has been updated this morning.
examples.zh: 核准供應商的清單已於今天早上更新。

examples.en: The manager, along with two assistants, is attending the conference.
examples.zh: 經理連同兩位助理正在出席這場會議。

toeic.pattern: Subject-verb agreement with collective nouns (集合名詞的主謂一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: 集合名詞(team，staff，committee，board 等)視為單一整體時用單數動詞，強調個別成員時可用複數。
toeic.structure: Collective N + 單數V (視為整體) / 複數V (強調成員) (整體用單數)

examples.en: The committee has reached a unanimous decision on the merger.
examples.zh: 委員會已就合併案達成一致決議。

examples.en: The staff are divided over the new remote-work policy.
examples.zh: 員工對新的遠距工作政策意見分歧。

toeic.pattern: Either / neither / none as subject: verb agreement (either / neither / none 作主詞之動詞一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: either/neither 當主詞接單數動詞；none of 後接不可數或視角不同時可單可複，正式商務多用單數。
toeic.structure: Neither / Either (+ of + N) + 單數V (正式用單數)

examples.en: Neither of the proposals meets our budget requirements.
examples.zh: 這兩份提案都不符合我們的預算要求。

examples.en: Either option is acceptable to the board.
examples.zh: 這兩個選項董事會都能接受。

toeic.pattern: There + be agreement with following noun (there be 句型與後接名詞一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: there be 句型的動詞單複數取決於其後緊接的名詞，而非句子真正主語的習慣。
toeic.structure: There + be (依其後名詞) + N (動詞配後接名詞)

examples.en: There is a significant delay in the shipment schedule.
examples.zh: 出貨時程有嚴重延誤。

examples.en: There are several issues we need to address before launch.
examples.zh: 上線前有幾個問題我們必須處理。

toeic.pattern: Indefinite pronouns as subject: everyone / someone / each (不定代名詞作主詞之動詞一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: everyone，someone，anybody，each，every 等不定代名詞當主詞時一律接單數動詞。
toeic.structure: Everyone / Each / Somebody + 單數V (一律單數)

examples.en: Everyone on the team is expected to submit a weekly report.
examples.zh: 團隊中每個人都須繳交週報。

examples.en: Each of the candidates was interviewed twice.
examples.zh: 每位應徵者都接受了兩次面試。

toeic.pattern: Noun clause with question word: how / why / when (疑問詞引導之名詞子句)
toeic.meaning_zh: 以疑問詞(how，why，when，where)引導的名詞子句作受詞時，使用直述語序而非疑問語序。
toeic.structure: S + V + how / why / when + S + V (直述語序) (間接問句不倒裝)

examples.en: Please explain why the invoice was issued twice.
examples.zh: 請說明為何這張發票被開立了兩次。

examples.en: We are not sure when the new branch will open.
examples.zh: 我們不確定新分店何時開幕。

toeic.pattern: Adverbial clause of concession: no matter / whatever (讓步副詞子句)
toeic.meaning_zh: no matter how/what/who 與 whatever/however 引導讓步副詞子句，表「無論…」。
toeic.structure: No matter + how / what + S + V, S + V (讓步副詞子句)

examples.en: No matter how busy you are, please reply to client emails within 24 hours.
examples.zh: 無論你多忙，請在二十四小時內回覆客戶郵件。

examples.en: Whatever the outcome may be, we will inform all stakeholders.
examples.zh: 無論結果如何，我們都會通知所有利害關係人。

toeic.pattern: Adverbial clause of manner: as / as if (方式副詞子句)
toeic.meaning_zh: as 引導方式副詞子句表「依照…的方式」，與商務指示常搭配。
toeic.structure: S + V + as + S + V (方式副詞子句)

examples.en: Please complete the form as the instructions indicate.
examples.zh: 請依照說明指示填寫表格。

examples.en: The project proceeded exactly as we had planned.
examples.zh: 這個專案完全照我們的計畫進行。

toeic.pattern: Distinguishing adjective vs adverb placement (形容詞與副詞位置辨析)
toeic.meaning_zh: 形容詞修飾名詞、副詞修飾動詞/形容詞/副詞；TOEIC 常考空格詞性判斷。
toeic.structure: Adj + N (修飾名詞) / V + Adv (修飾動詞) (詞性對應)

examples.en: The team handled the complaint professionally.
examples.zh: 團隊以專業的方式處理了這項投訴。

examples.en: We need a reliable supplier for this component.
examples.zh: 這個零件我們需要一家可靠的供應商。

toeic.pattern: Adverbs of degree with adjectives: highly / deeply / widely (程度副詞與形容詞搭配)
toeic.meaning_zh: 特定強調副詞與固定形容詞搭配(highly skilled，widely known，deeply concerned)，屬商務常見搭配。
toeic.structure: degree Adv + Adj (固定搭配) (副詞與形容詞搭配)

examples.en: Our company is highly regarded in the logistics industry.
examples.zh: 我們公司在物流業頗受推崇。

examples.en: The brand is widely recognized across Asia.
examples.zh: 這個品牌在亞洲廣為人知。

toeic.pattern: Order of adjectives: royal order (形容詞排列順序)
toeic.meaning_zh: 多個形容詞修飾同一名詞時依固定順序排列:意見-大小-年齡-形狀-顏色-來源-材質-用途。
toeic.structure: opinion + size + age + colour + origin + material + N (形容詞固定順序)

examples.en: They launched an innovative large-scale marketing campaign.
examples.zh: 他們推出了一項創新的大規模行銷活動。

examples.en: We ordered three small black leather chairs for the lobby.
examples.zh: 我們為大廳訂購了三張黑色小皮椅。

toeic.pattern: Possessive form: apostrophe vs of-phrase (所有格：撇號與 of 片語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 有生命/組織用 's 表所有格，無生命物多用 of-phrase；商務文件常見 company's policy 與 the cost of。
toeic.structure: N's + N (有生命 / 組織) / the + N + of + N (無生命) (所有格選擇)

examples.en: The company's annual report will be released next week.
examples.zh: 公司的年度報告將於下週發布。

examples.en: We are reviewing the terms of the contract.
examples.zh: 我們正在審查這份合約的條款。

toeic.pattern: Reflexive pronouns: myself / themselves (反身代名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 當動作主受詞相同時用反身代名詞，亦可表強調(親自)。
toeic.structure: S + V + 反身代名詞 (主受同一) / S + 反身代名詞 (強調) (反身 / 強調)

examples.en: The new hires familiarized themselves with the safety procedures.
examples.zh: 新進員工讓自己熟悉了安全程序。

examples.en: The CEO addressed the concerns herself.
examples.zh: 執行長親自處理了這些疑慮。

toeic.pattern: Demonstratives: this / that / these / those for reference (指示詞作指涉)
toeic.meaning_zh: 指示詞除指物外，在商務寫作中常用 that of / those of 避免名詞重複。
toeic.structure: that of + N (單數替代) / those of + N (複數替代) (避免名詞重複)

examples.en: Our sales figures this quarter exceeded those of last year.
examples.zh: 我們本季的銷售數字超越了去年的數字。

examples.en: The performance of the new model surpasses that of its predecessor.
examples.zh: 新型號的表現超越了前一代的表現。

toeic.pattern: One / ones as substitute pronoun (one / ones 替代代名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: one/ones 替代前面提過的可數名詞，避免重複，常與形容詞搭配。
toeic.structure: Adj + one / ones (替代可數名詞) (替代名詞)

examples.en: These printers are outdated; we should invest in newer ones.
examples.zh: 這些印表機過時了，我們應該投資較新的。

examples.en: If this plan fails, we will need a backup one.
examples.zh: 如果這個計畫失敗，我們會需要一個備案。

toeic.pattern: Compound nouns: noun + noun (複合名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 兩個名詞組合時前者作修飾語通常用單數形(customer service，sales report)。
toeic.structure: N (單數修飾) + N (主名詞) (前名詞用單數)

examples.en: Please forward this to the customer service department.
examples.zh: 請將此轉交客戶服務部門。

examples.en: The sales report is due by Friday.
examples.zh: 銷售報告週五前要交。

toeic.pattern: Countable vs uncountable nouns: information / equipment / advice (可數與不可數名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 部分商務名詞(information，equipment，advice，furniture，luggage)為不可數，無複數且接單數動詞。
toeic.structure: a piece of / some + 不可數N + 單數V (不可數名詞)

examples.en: The equipment needs to be inspected before use.
examples.zh: 這些設備使用前須經檢查。

examples.en: She gave me some valuable advice on the negotiation.
examples.zh: 她在談判方面給了我一些寶貴的建議。

toeic.pattern: Determiner agreement: this / these with noun number (限定詞與名詞數一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: 限定詞 this/that 配單數名詞，these/those 配複數名詞，須與名詞數一致。
toeic.structure: this / that + 單數N; these / those + 複數N (限定詞配名詞數)

examples.en: This document requires the director's signature.
examples.zh: 這份文件需要主管簽名。

examples.en: These figures do not match our records.
examples.zh: 這些數字與我們的紀錄不符。

toeic.pattern: Comparative with modifiers: much / far / slightly + comparative (比較級的程度修飾語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 程度副詞(much，far，slightly，considerably，a lot)可修飾比較級加強或減弱程度。
toeic.structure: much / far / slightly + 比較級 + than (修飾比較級)

examples.en: The new software is far more efficient than the old version.
examples.zh: 新軟體比舊版本有效率得多。

examples.en: Shipping costs are slightly higher this year.
examples.zh: 今年的運費略高一些。

toeic.pattern: Superlative with restrictive phrase: one of the + superlative (最高級之 one of the 句型)
toeic.meaning_zh: one of the + 最高級 + 複數名詞，表「最…之一」，動詞依主詞 one 用單數。
toeic.structure: one of the + 最高級 + 複數N + 單數V (最…之一)

examples.en: This is one of the most reliable suppliers in the region.
examples.zh: 這是該地區最可靠的供應商之一。

examples.en: She is one of the best negotiators on our team.
examples.zh: 她是我們團隊中最優秀的談判者之一。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: as + adjective + as for equality (as...as 表同等比較)
toeic.meaning_zh: as + 原級形容詞/副詞 + as 表兩者相等；否定 not as...as 表不及。
toeic.structure: as + 原級 + as; not as / so + 原級 + as (否定) (同級比較)

examples.en: This year's revenue is as strong as last year's.
examples.zh: 今年的營收和去年一樣強勁。

examples.en: The substitute is not as durable as the original part.
examples.zh: 替代品不如原廠零件耐用。

toeic.pattern: Comparison: prefer A to B / prefer to do rather than (prefer 表偏好)
toeic.meaning_zh: prefer 表偏好:prefer A to B(名詞)、prefer to do rather than do(動作)。
toeic.structure: prefer + N + to + N; prefer to + V + rather than + V (原形) (偏好結構)

examples.en: Most clients prefer email to phone calls for updates.
examples.zh: 多數客戶偏好以電子郵件而非電話接收最新消息。

examples.en: We prefer to negotiate directly rather than go through an agent.
examples.zh: 我們偏好直接談判而非透過代理。

toeic.pattern: Too...to vs so...that: replacement (too...to 與 so...that 互換)
toeic.meaning_zh: too + adj + to V 表「太…以致不能」，語意等同 so + adj + that S can't，常互換考。
toeic.structure: too + adj + to + V = so + adj + that + S + cannot + V (否定結果)

examples.en: The deadline is too tight to meet without overtime.
examples.zh: 這個期限太緊，不加班無法達成。

examples.en: The instructions were too vague to follow.
examples.zh: 這些說明太模糊，無法照做。

toeic.pattern: Enough as determiner vs adverb (enough 作限定詞與副詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: enough 置於名詞前作限定詞(enough staff)，置於形容詞/副詞後作副詞(good enough)。
toeic.structure: enough + N (限定詞) / adj + enough (副詞) (位置依詞性)

examples.en: We do not have enough staff to cover the holiday shifts.
examples.zh: 我們沒有足夠的人手來支應假日輪班。

examples.en: The prototype is not durable enough for mass production.
examples.zh: 這個原型不夠耐用，無法量產。

toeic.pattern: Such / so with noun and adjective (such / so 接名詞與形容詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: so + 形容詞/副詞；such + (a/an) + 形容詞 + 名詞，表程度強調。
toeic.structure: so + adj / adv; such + (a / an) + adj + N (程度強調)

examples.en: The response was so positive that we extended the promotion.
examples.zh: 反應如此熱烈，我們因此延長了促銷。

examples.en: It was such a successful event that we will hold it annually.
examples.zh: 這是如此成功的活動，我們將每年舉辦。

toeic.pattern: Articles with proper and institutional nouns (專有與機構名詞的冠詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 特定機構、地理名稱、報紙等須用 the(the United Nations，the Times)，國名單數一般不加。
toeic.structure: the + 機構 / 海洋 / 報刊; zero article + 國名 / 街名 (專有名詞冠詞)

examples.en: The board reported the findings to the United Nations committee.
examples.zh: 董事會向聯合國委員會報告了調查結果。

examples.en: Our headquarters relocated to Wall Street last year.
examples.zh: 我們的總部去年遷至華爾街。

toeic.pattern: Zero article in fixed prepositional phrases (固定介系詞片語的零冠詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 許多固定介系詞片語省略冠詞(by car，in person，on time，at work，in charge)。
toeic.structure: prep + 名詞 (無冠詞固定片語) (固定片語省冠詞)

examples.en: The client prefers to sign the contract in person.
examples.zh: 客戶偏好親自簽署合約。

examples.en: The technician currently in charge is on leave.
examples.zh: 目前負責的技術人員正在休假。

toeic.pattern: Quantifier: a number of vs the amount of (a number of 與 the amount of)
toeic.meaning_zh: a number of + 可數複數名詞(接複數動詞)；the amount of + 不可數名詞(接單數動詞)。
toeic.structure: a number of + 複數N + 複數V; the amount of + 不可數N + 單數V (可數 / 不可數)

examples.en: A number of employees have requested flexible hours.
examples.zh: 許多員工要求彈性工時。

examples.en: The amount of paperwork has increased significantly.
examples.zh: 文書作業量大幅增加。

toeic.pattern: Quantifier: most / most of / almost all (數量詞 most 的用法)
toeic.meaning_zh: most + 名詞(泛指)；most of the + 名詞(特指)；almost 修飾 all/every 而非直接接名詞。
toeic.structure: most + N; most of the + N; almost all + N (泛指 / 特指)

examples.en: Most customers value fast delivery over low prices.
examples.zh: 多數顧客重視快速配送勝過低價。

examples.en: Most of the complaints were resolved within a day.
examples.zh: 大部分的投訴在一天內獲得解決。

toeic.pattern: Distributives: every vs each vs all (分配詞 every / each / all)
toeic.meaning_zh: each 強調個別(接單數)，every 強調全體中的每一(接單數)，all 指整體(接複數/不可數)。
toeic.structure: each / every + 單數N + 單數V; all + 複數N + 複數V (個別 / 整體)

examples.en: Every applicant must complete an online assessment.
examples.zh: 每位應徵者都必須完成線上評量。

examples.en: All employees are entitled to annual leave.
examples.zh: 所有員工都享有年假。

toeic.pattern: Both / either / neither: two items (兩者用之 both / either / neither)
toeic.meaning_zh: 指兩者時:both 兩者皆(複數動詞)、either 任一(單數)、neither 兩者皆不(單數)。
toeic.structure: both + 複數V; either / neither + 單數V (限兩者)

examples.en: Both candidates are qualified for the position.
examples.zh: 兩位應徵者都符合這個職位資格。

examples.en: Neither branch was profitable last quarter.
examples.zh: 上一季兩家分店都沒有獲利。

toeic.pattern: Preposition vs conjunction: because of vs because (because of 與 because 辨析)
toeic.meaning_zh: because of/due to + 名詞片語；because + 完整子句。TOEIC 常考空格後接名詞或子句的判斷。
toeic.structure: because of + N; because + S + V (後接名詞 / 子句)

examples.en: The flight was delayed because of severe weather.
examples.zh: 班機因惡劣天候延誤。

examples.en: The flight was delayed because the weather was severe.
examples.zh: 班機延誤，因為天候惡劣。

toeic.pattern: Transitional phrase: as a result / in contrast / for instance (轉折連接片語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 連接性介系詞片語連結句子，商務寫作用於表結果、對比、舉例，後接逗號。
toeic.structure: As a result, S + V / In contrast, S + V (句首轉折片語)

examples.en: Sales dropped sharply; as a result, we revised our forecast.
examples.zh: 銷售驟降，因此我們修正了預測。

examples.en: For instance, our overseas orders doubled this year.
examples.zh: 舉例來說，我們的海外訂單今年翻倍。

toeic.pattern: Conditional connector: in case vs in case of (in case 與 in case of)
toeic.meaning_zh: in case + 子句(以防)；in case of + 名詞(萬一發生…時)。
toeic.structure: in case + S + V; in case of + N (後接子句 / 名詞)

examples.en: Keep a backup copy in case the system crashes.
examples.zh: 請保留備份，以防系統當機。

examples.en: In case of emergency, use the stairwell on the left.
examples.zh: 萬一發生緊急狀況，請使用左側樓梯。

toeic.pattern: Phrasal verbs: separable vs inseparable (可分與不可分片語動詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 可分式片語動詞受詞為代名詞時須置於動副之間(turn it off)；不可分式不可拆開。
toeic.structure: V + 代名詞 + adv (可分式); V + prep + N (不可分式) (可分 / 不可分)

examples.en: Please fill out the form and hand it in by noon.
examples.zh: 請填好表格並在中午前交出。

examples.en: We need to look into the cause of the delay.
examples.zh: 我們需要調查延誤的原因。

toeic.pattern: Verb + object + infinitive: want / expect / advise sb to do (動詞接受詞加不定詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 特定動詞接「受詞 + to 不定詞」結構表期望某人做某事(advise，encourage，expect，allow)。
toeic.structure: V + 受詞 + to + V (原形) (受詞+不定詞)

examples.en: The manager advised the team to revise the proposal.
examples.zh: 經理建議團隊修改提案。

examples.en: We encourage all staff to attend the training session.
examples.zh: 我們鼓勵全體員工參加這場培訓。

toeic.pattern: Verb + that-clause vs verb + to-infinitive: decide / agree / hope (動詞接 that 子句與不定詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 部分動詞可接 that 子句或 to 不定詞，語意略異(hope to do / hope that S V)。
toeic.structure: V + to + V (原形) / V + that + S + V (接不定詞或子句)

examples.en: The board agreed to postpone the launch.
examples.zh: 董事會同意延後上市。

examples.en: We hope that the new policy will boost morale.
examples.zh: 我們希望新政策能提振士氣。

toeic.pattern: It as preparatory subject: it is + adj + to / that (虛主詞 it)
toeic.meaning_zh: 用虛主詞 it 代替真正的不定詞或 that 子句主語，使句子結構平衡。
toeic.structure: It + be + adj + to + V / that + S + V (虛主詞)

examples.en: It is essential to confirm the order before shipping.
examples.zh: 出貨前確認訂單是必要的。

examples.en: It is unlikely that the deadline will be extended.
examples.zh: 期限不太可能延長。

toeic.pattern: It as preparatory object: find / make / consider it + adj (虛受詞 it)
toeic.meaning_zh: 動詞後用虛受詞 it 代替後面的不定詞或子句(find it difficult to do)。
toeic.structure: V + it + adj + to + V (虛受詞)

examples.en: We found it difficult to meet the new compliance standards.
examples.zh: 我們發現要符合新的合規標準很困難。

examples.en: The system makes it easy to track shipments in real time.
examples.zh: 這套系統讓即時追蹤貨運變得容易。

toeic.pattern: Bare infinitive after perception verbs: see / hear / watch sb do (感官動詞接原形動詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 感官動詞 see/hear/watch/feel 接受詞後用原形動詞表完整動作，用 V-ing 表進行中。
toeic.structure: V (感官) + 受詞 + V (原形 / V-ing) (原形=完整動作)

examples.en: We saw the delivery truck arrive at the warehouse.
examples.zh: 我們看到送貨卡車抵達倉庫。

examples.en: The supervisor heard the alarm going off during the night shift.
examples.zh: 主管聽到警報在夜班時響起。

toeic.pattern: Gerund as subject of sentence (動名詞作主詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 動名詞片語可當主詞，視為單數接單數動詞，商務寫作常見。
toeic.structure: V-ing + ... + 單數V (動名詞作主詞)

examples.en: Reducing operating costs remains our top priority.
examples.zh: 降低營運成本仍是我們的首要任務。

examples.en: Maintaining strong client relationships requires consistent effort.
examples.zh: 維持穩固的客戶關係需要持續的努力。

toeic.pattern: Gerund after prepositions (介系詞後接動名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 介系詞後接動詞一律用動名詞形式，不可用不定詞(interested in doing)。
toeic.structure: prep + V-ing (介系詞後接動名詞)

examples.en: The firm is committed to improving customer satisfaction.
examples.zh: 該公司致力於提升顧客滿意度。

examples.en: They succeeded in renegotiating the contract terms.
examples.zh: 他們成功重新協商了合約條款。

toeic.pattern: Infinitive as adjective complement: be + adj + to do (不定詞作形容詞補語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 形容詞後接 to 不定詞說明範圍或評價(be eager to，be ready to，be likely to)。
toeic.structure: be + adj + to + V (原形) (形容詞補語)

examples.en: The new recruits are eager to learn the workflow.
examples.zh: 新進人員渴望學習工作流程。

examples.en: The supplier was reluctant to lower the price.
examples.zh: 供應商不願意降價。

toeic.pattern: Relative pronoun with preposition: in which / for whom (介系詞加關係代名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 正式書面中介系詞可置於關係代名詞前(the company for which she works)。
toeic.structure: prep + which / whom + S + V (介系詞前移)

examples.en: This is the department in which most complaints originate.
examples.zh: 這是大多數投訴源自的部門。

examples.en: The client to whom we sent the proposal has not replied.
examples.zh: 我們寄出提案的那位客戶尚未回覆。

toeic.pattern: Quantifying relative clause: some of which / many of whom (量詞加關係代名詞之非限定子句)
toeic.meaning_zh: 量詞 + of which/whom 引導非限定關係子句，補充說明先行詞中的部分。
toeic.structure: N, + 量詞 + of which / whom + V (部分補述)

examples.en: We received fifty applications, many of which were impressive.
examples.zh: 我們收到五十份申請，其中許多令人印象深刻。

examples.en: The company has three branches, all of which are profitable.
examples.zh: 公司有三家分店，全都有獲利。

toeic.pattern: Pronoun-antecedent agreement (代名詞與先行詞一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: 代名詞須與其指涉的先行詞在人稱、數、性別上一致，常考單複數誤用。
toeic.structure: 先行詞 + ... + 代名詞 (數 / 性一致) (代名詞配先行詞)

examples.en: Each department must submit its budget by the end of the month.
examples.zh: 每個部門必須在月底前提交其預算。

examples.en: The employees received their bonuses last Friday.
examples.zh: 員工們上週五領到了他們的獎金。

toeic.pattern: Negative adverb frequency word order: rarely / seldom / hardly (頻率副詞語序)
toeic.meaning_zh: 頻率與程度副詞(rarely，seldom，hardly，often，always)通常置於主要動詞前、be 動詞後。
toeic.structure: S + (rarely / often) + V; S + be + (rarely) + ... (副詞位置)

examples.en: The director rarely approves last-minute requests.
examples.zh: 主管很少批准臨時提出的請求。

examples.en: Complaints are seldom ignored by our service team.
examples.zh: 我們的服務團隊很少忽視投訴。

toeic.pattern: Conjunction: whether...or not for choice (whether...or not 表無論是否)
toeic.meaning_zh: whether...or not 引導表「無論是否」的名詞或副詞子句。
toeic.structure: whether + S + V + or not (無論是否)

examples.en: Please confirm whether or not you can attend the meeting.
examples.zh: 請確認你是否能出席會議。

examples.en: Whether the deal closes or not, we will keep the client informed.
examples.zh: 無論這筆交易是否成交，我們都會持續通知客戶。

toeic.pattern: Sentence connector: not only at sentence start with inversion (not only 句首倒裝)
toeic.meaning_zh: not only 置於句首時主詞與助動詞須倒裝，強調除…之外還…。
toeic.structure: Not only + 助動詞 + S + V, but (also) S + V (句首倒裝)

examples.en: Not only did the new system reduce errors, but it also saved time.
examples.zh: 新系統不僅減少了錯誤，也節省了時間。

examples.en: Not only does she manage sales, but she also oversees marketing.
examples.zh: 她不僅管理銷售，也督導行銷。

toeic.pattern: Emphasis with do / does / did: emphatic verb (do / does / did 強調語氣)
toeic.meaning_zh: 在肯定句中加入助動詞 do/does/did 於原形動詞前，以強調語氣。
toeic.structure: S + do / does / did + V (原形) (強調動作)

examples.en: We do appreciate your patience during the system upgrade.
examples.zh: 我們確實感謝您在系統升級期間的耐心。

examples.en: The report did meet all the client's requirements.
examples.zh: 這份報告的確符合了客戶的所有要求。

toeic.pattern: Fronting for emphasis: object / adverbial fronting (受詞或副詞前置強調)
toeic.meaning_zh: 將受詞或副詞片語移至句首以強調，常見於正式商務語境。
toeic.structure: 受詞 / 副詞片語 + S + V (成分前置強調)

examples.en: This issue we must resolve before the audit.
examples.zh: 這個問題我們必須在稽核前解決。

examples.en: Only after careful review will the contract be signed.
examples.zh: 唯有經過仔細審查，合約才會簽署。

toeic.pattern: Apposition: noun phrase restating noun (同位語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 同位語以名詞片語進一步說明前一名詞，通常以逗號隔開。
toeic.structure: N, + 名詞片語 (同位語), + V (同位語補充)

examples.en: Ms. Lin, our regional director, will lead the negotiation.
examples.zh: 我們的區域總監林女士將主持這次談判。

examples.en: The Q3 report, a key document for investors, is now available.
examples.zh: 第三季報告，一份投資人的重要文件，現已公布。

toeic.pattern: Absolute phrase: independent participial construction (獨立分詞構句)
toeic.meaning_zh: 獨立分詞構句帶自己的主詞，修飾整句，常見於正式商務描述。
toeic.structure: N + V-ing / p.p., + S + V (獨立分詞構句)

examples.en: The agenda finalized, the committee adjourned the meeting.
examples.zh: 議程確定後，委員會結束了會議。

examples.en: All issues being resolved, the project moved into the next phase.
examples.zh: 所有問題都解決後，專案進入下一階段。

toeic.pattern: Verb patterns: spend / waste + time / money + V-ing (spend / waste 接動名詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: spend/waste + 時間或金錢 + (in) V-ing，表花費於某事。
toeic.structure: spend / waste + 時間 / 金錢 + (in) + V-ing (花費於)

examples.en: We spent three weeks finalizing the merger agreement.
examples.zh: 我們花了三週敲定合併協議。

examples.en: Do not waste resources duplicating existing reports.
examples.zh: 不要把資源浪費在重複製作既有報告上。

toeic.pattern: Comparative parallelism: more A than B / less A than B (比較級平行結構)
toeic.meaning_zh: 比較兩個性質時 more...than / less...than 兩側結構須平行對等。
toeic.structure: more + A + than + B (結構對等) (平行比較)

examples.en: The proposal is more about cost-cutting than about growth.
examples.zh: 這份提案重點在於削減成本而非成長。

examples.en: Clients are less concerned about price than about reliability.
examples.zh: 客戶在意可靠度更勝於價格。

toeic.pattern: Range and approximation expressions: between...and / from...to (範圍與約略表達)
toeic.meaning_zh: 表數值範圍用 between X and Y、from X to Y、ranging from，商務報表常見。
toeic.structure: between + X + and + Y; from + X + to + Y (範圍表達)

examples.en: Delivery usually takes between three and five business days.
examples.zh: 配送通常需要三到五個工作天。

examples.en: Prices range from $20 to $50 depending on the model.
examples.zh: 價格依型號從二十美元到五十美元不等。

toeic.pattern: Percentage and fraction agreement (百分比與分數的動詞一致)
toeic.meaning_zh: 分數或百分比作主詞時，動詞依 of 後名詞的單複數決定。
toeic.structure: 分數 / % + of + N + V (依N單複數) (動詞配 of 名詞)

examples.en: Forty percent of the budget has been allocated to marketing.
examples.zh: 百分之四十的預算已分配給行銷。

examples.en: Two-thirds of the employees prefer hybrid work.
examples.zh: 三分之二的員工偏好混合辦公。

toeic.pattern: Negative question and confirmation (否定疑問句與確認)
toeic.meaning_zh: 否定疑問句用於確認或表驚訝，商務口語常見，回答依事實而非問句形式。
toeic.structure: Aux + not + S + V? (否定確認)

examples.en: Didn't we already send the invoice last week?
examples.zh: 我們上週不是已經寄出發票了嗎?

examples.en: Isn't the meeting scheduled for Thursday?
examples.zh: 會議不是排在週四嗎?

toeic.pattern: Future time clause: present tense after when / as soon as (未來時間子句用現在式)
toeic.meaning_zh: 表未來的時間副詞子句中(when，as soon as，before，until)用現在式代替未來式。
toeic.structure: S + will + V, when / as soon as + S + V (現在式) (時間子句用現在式)

examples.en: We will ship the order as soon as payment is confirmed.
examples.zh: 一收到付款確認，我們就會出貨。

examples.en: Please notify me before the client arrives.
examples.zh: 客戶抵達前請通知我。

toeic.pattern: So / neither for agreement: so do I / neither does he (so / neither 表附和)
toeic.meaning_zh: 附和肯定用 so + 助動詞 + 主詞；附和否定用 neither/nor + 助動詞 + 主詞，須倒裝。
toeic.structure: So + 助動詞 + S (肯定附和); Neither + 助動詞 + S (否定附和) (附和倒裝)

examples.en: Our supplier raised prices, and so did our competitors.
examples.zh: 我們的供應商漲價了，我們的競爭對手也是。

examples.en: The CEO didn't attend, and neither did the CFO.
examples.zh: 執行長沒出席，財務長也沒有。

toeic.pattern: Adjective clauses reduced to phrases: which is / who are omission (關係子句簡化為片語)
toeic.meaning_zh: 關係代名詞 + be 動詞可省略，留下分詞或介系詞片語修飾名詞。
toeic.structure: N + (which / who + be 省略) + p.p. / prep 片語 (關係子句精簡)

examples.en: The candidates selected for the final round will be contacted.
examples.zh: 獲選進入決選的應徵者將會接到通知。

examples.en: All documents relevant to the case must be archived.
examples.zh: 所有與本案相關的文件都須歸檔。

toeic.pattern: Conjunction: as long as / so long as for condition (as long as 表條件)
toeic.meaning_zh: as long as 引導條件子句表「只要」，子句用現在式表未來條件。
toeic.structure: as long as + S + V (現在式), S + will + V (條件子句)

examples.en: We will extend the warranty as long as you register the product.
examples.zh: 只要您登錄產品，我們就會延長保固。

examples.en: You may use the facility as long as you follow the rules.
examples.zh: 只要遵守規定，您就可以使用這項設施。

toeic.pattern: Indirect question word order in requests (請求中間接問句語序)
toeic.meaning_zh: 禮貌請求中嵌入間接問句(Could you tell me where...)，內部用直述語序。
toeic.structure: Could you tell me + 疑問詞 + S + V (直述語序) (間接問句語序)

examples.en: Could you tell me where the conference room is located?
examples.zh: 能否告訴我會議室在哪裡?

examples.en: I would like to know how the refund process works.
examples.zh: 我想了解退款流程是如何運作的。

toeic.pattern: Conjunction: now that for reason with result (now that 表既然原因)
toeic.meaning_zh: now that 引導表「既然/由於現在…」的原因子句，強調已成立的新狀況。
toeic.structure: now that + S + V, S + V (既然)

examples.en: Now that the contract is signed, we can begin production.
examples.zh: 既然合約已簽，我們就能開始生產。

examples.en: Now that sales have recovered, we will resume hiring.
examples.zh: 既然銷售已回升，我們將恢復招聘。

toeic.pattern: Conjunction: in order that / so that for purpose with clause (so that 表目的子句)
toeic.meaning_zh: so that / in order that 引導目的子句，後接含 can/will/may 的完整子句。
toeic.structure: S + V + so that + S + can / will + V (目的子句)

examples.en: We upgraded the servers so that the site can handle more traffic.
examples.zh: 我們升級了伺服器，以便網站能承載更多流量。

examples.en: Send the file early so that the team has time to review it.
examples.zh: 提早寄送檔案，讓團隊有時間審閱。

toeic.pattern: Adverbial use of present participle: resulting in / leading to (現在分詞作結果副詞)
toeic.meaning_zh: 現在分詞片語作結果副詞，表前一動作的後續結果(resulting in，leading to)。
toeic.structure: S + V, + V-ing (結果) + N (分詞表結果)

examples.en: Sales surged in the third quarter, resulting in record profits.
examples.zh: 第三季銷售激增，帶來了創紀錄的利潤。

examples.en: The merger expanded our network, leading to new opportunities.
examples.zh: 這次合併擴大了我們的網絡，帶來新的機會。
